problem.title: Why Egyptians dread their
problem.subtitle: Crossing just one tier costs hundreds of pounds. Kashf makes sure it never happens.

without.title: Without Kashf
without.1: Bill arrived — 847 EGP instead of the expected 350 EGP
without.2: Jumped to Tier 5 mid-month with zero warning
without.3: AC, water heater, and appliances all running simultaneously
without.4: No visibility into daily usage, no forecast, no way out
without.billNote: Tier 5 applied — 0.72 EGP/kWh on last 300 kWh

with.title: With Kashf
with.1: Real-time tier tracking shows you're at 287 kWh, 63 left to Tier 5
with.2: 48-hour forecast: 'You'll cross at this rate within 2.5 days'
with.3: AI sent an alert: Reduce AC by 1.5 hrs for 3 days
with.4: End-of-month bill: 412 EGP instead of 847 EGP — saved 435 EGP
with.billNote: Stayed in Tier 3 — saved 435 EGP this month

problem.bannerSuffix: by staying ahead of tier thresholds.

ai.title: Personalized tips in
ai.desc: Kashf's AI analyzes your consumption profile and gives you actionable steps in Egyptian Arabic — not generic advice, but recommendations based on your home's actual behavior.

chat.msg1: Moving to the next tier will cost you 187 EGP extra. Reducing AC by 2 hours a day saves 64 EGP.
chat.msg2: What's better — change the AC temperature or reduce run time?
chat.msg3: Raising the temp to 26°C is better — saves more and you won't notice a big difference. Try it tonight!

hero.title: Helping Families Take Control of Their
hero.desc: Kashf was built to solve a growing challenge facing Egyptian households: unexpected electricity bill increases caused by crossing into higher Sheriha tiers. Our mission is to make electricity consumption simple, transparent, and actionable.

story.title: Built for
story.body1: In Egypt, many families only discover they exceeded their electricity tier after receiving an expensive monthly bill. The tiered Sheriha system means crossing a threshold by even 1 kWh can dramatically increase costs.
story.body2: Kashf was created to provide proactive insights, helping users understand their consumption before costly surprises happen. Our AI-powered platform analyzes usage patterns, predicts tier crossings, and delivers personalized recommendations.
story.body3: We’re focused on the Egyptian market, understanding local tariff structures, consumption patterns, and household needs. Our mission is simple: empower families with transparency and control.

t1.label: The Problem
t1.desc: Egyptian families face unpredictable electricity bills

t2.label: The Solution
t2.desc: Kashf is born with AI-powered insights

values.title: Four principles
values.subtitle: Our compass for building a product Egyptians trust with their energy and money.

v1.title: Accuracy First
v1.desc: We obsess over OCR precision and prediction quality. Every decimal point matters when it affects your bill.

v2.title: Built for Egypt
v2.desc: Kashf is designed around Egypt’s tariff structure, spoken Arabic, and real household usage patterns.

v3.title: Community-Driven
v3.desc: Every feature starts with real feedback from Egyptian families who use Kashf daily.

v4.title: Speed & Simplicity
v4.desc: Scan your meter in seconds. See your forecast instantly. No spreadsheets, no complexity.

team.title: The people
team.subtitle: A small, dedicated team of Egyptians passionate about energy transparency and smart technology.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before getting started with Kashf.
faq.contact: Still have questions? Email us at

q1.a: Kashf is an AI-powered electricity consumption assistant built for Egyptian households. It reads your meter, tracks your Sheriha tier in real time, forecasts your bill, and gives personalized Arabic-language recommendations to help you save money every month.

q2.a: Yes — Kashf is completely free during the beta period. No credit card required. Once we exit beta, a generous free tier will always remain available.

q3.q: How does the meter scanning work?
q3.a: You simply point your phone camera at your electricity meter. Kashf’s OCR engine reads the digits in real time and stores the reading securely. No special hardware needed — any modern smartphone works.

q4.a: Egypt’s electricity pricing is divided into consumption tiers (شرائح). The more kWh you use, the higher your price per unit. Crossing into a higher tier mid-month can cost hundreds of extra pounds. Kashf tracks your exact position and warns you before you cross.

q5.a: Yes. Kashf is a Progressive Web App (PWA) with offline-first architecture. You can log readings and view your dashboard without an internet connection. Data syncs automatically when you reconnect.

q6.q: Is my data private and secure?
q6.a: Absolutely. Your data is encrypted in transit and at rest. We never sell or share your consumption data with third parties. You can delete your account and all data at any time.

q7.q: Can I install it without an app store?
q7.a: Yes. Visit kashf.app in your browser, tap ‘Add to Home Screen’, and Kashf installs just like a native app — on iOS, Android, Windows, and macOS — with no app store required.
